channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: How the Butler shooting changed Donald Trump's campaign
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Donald Trump was onstage at a rally on Long Island last month, talking about taxes, when he appeared momentarily spooked by something he had spotted over his shoulder. “I thought this was a wise guy coming up,” he explained, joking that he was getting his elbow ready to fight back.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden pledged to campaign hard for Harris. So far, he's been mostly a no-show
item.description: When President Joe Biden ended his 2024 campaign in July and endorsed Kamala Harris, he pledged to be out campaigning for her to succeed him.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert And Bill Barrow, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Major cases before the Supreme Court deal with transgender rights, guns, nuclear waste and vapes
item.description: The Supreme Court’s new term is opening with a handful of important cases set to be heard and the possibility that the justices will be asked to get involved in election disputes.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The Supreme Court opens its new term with election disputes in the air but not yet on the docket
item.description: Transgender rights, the regulation of “ghost guns” and the death penalty highlight the Supreme Court’s election-season term that begins Monday.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sherman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A Texas execution is renewing calls for clemency. It's rarely granted
item.description: Texas rarely grants clemency for death row inmates, but that's not stopping a group of bipartisan lawmakers from showing their support for a man scheduled to be executed in October.
item.dc:creator: Nadia Lathan And Summer Ballentine, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Florida voters concerned about election disruptions after Hurricane Helene
item.description: Polling sites along the coast have lost locations due to Hurricane Helene's damage, and Florida voters are starting to worry about how this could change things for the 2024 election.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Harris, Trump campaigns send surrogates to Las Vegas to court middle class, Latinos
item.description: With a month until Election Day, Vice President Kamala Harris and former President Donald Trump sent their surrogates to Las Vegas this week to campaign for Nevada’s six electoral votes.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: China could wage economic war on Taiwan to force surrender, report says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — U.S. military officials and analysts have for years warned of possible armed attacks or blockades by China on Taiwan, but a report released Friday has raised a red flag about possible non-military tactics that could be used effectively against the self-governed island.
item.dc:creator: DIDI TANG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Taiwanese people ready to fight as China ramps up aggression, ambassador says
item.description: Taiwan's representative to the U.S., Alexander Yui, is vowing that the island's people are ready to fight if China invades the island.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Blackface photo shakes up toss-up House district in NY
item.description: New York Republican Congressman Michael Lawler is embroiled in controversy over a 2006 Halloween costume, when he dressed in blackface as Michael Jackson.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House Republican apologizes for wearing blackface at 2006 Halloween party
item.description: Rep. Mike Lawler (R-N.Y.) apologized late Thursday night for wearing blackface as part of a Halloween costume portraying pop star Michael Jackson in 2006 during a college party.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris to tour Helene damage in North Carolina on Saturday
item.description: Vice President Harris will travel Saturday to North Carolina to tour the damage from Hurricane Helene and get an update on the ongoing recovery efforts in the state.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Assemblyman Eddie Gibbs arrested during traffic stop: NYPD
item.description: EAST HARLEM, Manhattan (PIX11) – New York state Assemblyman Eddie Gibbs was arrested during a traffic stop in Manhattan on Thursday, according to the NYPD. The East Harlem Democrat was taken into custody in front of 1844 Lexington Ave. around 1:30 p.m., police said.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Trump's campaign says he raised $160 million in September, ended month with $283 million banked
item.description: Former President Donald Trump raised $160 million for his campaign in September and entered October with $283 million in the bank for the campaign's final sprint, his aides announced.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Bipartisan California lawmakers urge House leaders to boost disaster relief funding
item.description: A bipartisan group of lawmakers from Orange County, Calif., are urging House leaders to include disaster relief funding in fiscal 2025 spending, highlighting wildfire damage in the state even as damage from Hurricane Helene on the other side of the country has heightened attention on the issue.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Programming alert: NewsNation to feature PA Senate race tonight
item.description: NewsNation will have live coverage of the Pennsylvania senate debate between Bob Casey and David McCormick.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Voters in 13 states have less than a week to register. When is the deadline in Illinois?
item.description: The 2024 presidential election is fast approaching, and in some states, the voter registration deadline is as soon as this week.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, WGN 9

item.title: Harris-Walz campaign kicks off a nationwide effort to reach Native voters
item.description: Tribal leaders from across the country showed their support for the democratic ticket at Mystic Lake Event Center in Prior Lake on Wednesday. Native American civic leaders say the Harris-Walz campaign is prepared to support issues that impact Indigenous communities.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Olson, MPR News

item.title: NJ lawmakers explore why summer energy bills were so high
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (PIX11) - If your energy bills were sky-high this summer, you are not alone. It's why New Jersey lawmakers came together for a hearing to learn why and to discuss how to solve it.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Cook County State's Attorney candidate Former Judge Eileen O’Neill Burke on crime, guns and youth violence
item.description: In deep blue Cook County, the Democratic candidate for states attorney, retired Judge Eileen O’Neill Burke, is looking past the November election and sounding like she’s already on the job.
item.dc:creator: Tahman Bradley, WGN 9

item.title: Meet the candidates running for San Diego City Attorney
item.description: There are only two candidates in the running to become San Diego City Attorney after incumbent Mara Elliott ends her last term in office in December.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: 5 takeaways from the Vance-Walz VP debate
item.description: Ohio Sen. JD Vance (R) and Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D) met face-to-face in New York City on Tuesday in the first and likely only vice presidential debate of the election cycle, a high-stakes moment that comes a little more than a month before Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: What we gleaned from key moments of the Walz-Vance debate in New York
item.description: MPR News senior politics reporter Dana Ferguson is in New York with takeaways from the only debate between the vice presidential hopefuls.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer, Dana Ferguson, and Aleesa Kuznetsov, MPR News

item.title: Vance, Walz address school shootings
item.description: Walz said the solution is more research into gun violence. Vance said we have to increase security in our schools.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: NPR fact checked the Vance-Walz vice presidential debate. Here's what we found
item.description: JD Vance and Tim Walz debated on Tuesday in the last scheduled debate of the election. The sparring was mostly collegial but a number of points on key issues require additional context or corrections.
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff, MPR News

item.title: What to know on claims made about Minnesota, Walz in VP debate against Vance
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz’s home state of Minnesota came up early in his debate against fellow vice presidential candidate Ohio Sen. JD Vance on Tuesday evening. Here are the facts.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: LIVE UPDATES: Vance, Walz vice presidential debate
item.description: Vice presidential nominees J.D. Vance and Tim Walz are facing off for the first -- and likely only -- time before the 2024 election, which is just over a month away.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Are convicted felons allowed to vote in Illinois?
item.description: With just five weeks until the 2024 presidential election, most U.S. citizens over the age of 18 are eligible to vote as long as they are registered. However, for some individuals, the right to vote may be more complicated due to a criminal conviction.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, WGN 9

item.title: Trump takes lead over Harris in North Carolina Quinnipiac survey
item.description: Trump leads with 49 percent support, Harris earns 47 percent support, Green Party candidate Jill Stein gets 1 percent support and Justice For All Party candidate Cornel West gets 1 percent support in the Quinnipiac University poll conducted in late September and released Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Volunteers from deep blue Illinois learn to canvas in battleground states
item.description: Across the seven states likely to decide the presidential election, thousands of volunteers, including some from Illinois, are on the ground going door-to-door working to increase voter turnout.
item.dc:creator: Tahman Bradley, WGN 9

item.title: U.S. Fish and Wildlife signs Tamarac co-stewardship agreement with White Earth
item.description: The U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service and the White Earth Nation have signed a co-stewardship agreement for the Tamarac National Wildlife Refuge in northwestern Minnesota. The agreement will not change public access to the refuge, but will give tribal resource managers more input in refuge management.
item.dc:creator: Dan Gunderson, MPR News

item.title: JD Vance, Tim Walz face off in 2024 VP debate: Key takeaways
item.description: Republican J.D. Vance and Democrat Tim Walz met Tuesday in the lone vice presidential debate of the 2024 election, bringing together undercards who have spent two months going after each other and the opposing nominees who top the major-party tickets.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WGN 9

item.title: Should moderators fact-check? Veteran broadcast debate host, journalism professor weigh in
item.description: MPR News host emeritus Gary Eichten and University of Minnesota associate professor and director of the Minnesota Journalism Center Benjamin Toff joined Morning Edition host Cathy Wurzer to talk about the positives and perils of live political debates.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Reviewing the Vance-Walz vice-presidential debate
item.description: MPR News host Angela Davis talks with political analysts about Tuesday night’s vice-presidential debate between Ohio Sen. JD Vance and Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz.
item.dc:creator: Angela Davis and Cari Dwyer, MPR News

item.title: Trump suggests ‘1 rough hour’ of policing would end retail crime
item.description: The former president called for law enforcement to be freed up to "do their job" without restrictions.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: What to know about the Walz-Vance debate
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D) and Sen. JD Vance (R-Ohio) are set to go head-to-head on Tuesday night in their first and likely only debate as vice presidential contenders.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, Fox 5

item.title: Trump suspect pleads not guilty in attempted assassination
item.description: The man accused of attempting to assassinate former President Trump at one of his Florida golf courses earlier this month has pleaded not guilty to five federal charges, according to The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WFLA, NBC
